title: Design System: Project Amazon PH Academy v2

display.purpose: Marketing hero only. One per page, maximum.

body.purpose: Default reading copy, 72ch max for lessons, 60ch for UI text.

label.purpose: Buttons, form labels, nav items, table headers.

mono.purpose: Tabular numbers, bid values, hashes, code.

shadows.name: shadow-sm
shadows.purpose: Ambient lift on interactive card hover.

shadows.name: shadow-lg
shadows.purpose: Modals — the deepest shadow in the system.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default easing — content appearing, modals opening.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one high-emphasis action per view. Dark ink text on Waybill Orange, presses down 1px on click.
components.css: .ds-btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 0 16px; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), transform 80ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn:active { transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #FF6B35; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary { background: #FF6B35; color: #1A1A2E; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #E55A2B; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Bordered, white background — the fallback action beside a primary button.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #FFFFFF; color: #171717; border: 1px solid #E5E5E0; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #F4F3EE; border-color: #A3A3A3; }

components.name: Default Card
components.description: Static card. Border is the elevation; no shadow at rest.
components.html:
Module 3: Bid Strategy

Learn how to read search term reports and adjust bids with confidence.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #E5E5E0; border-radius: 10px; padding: 16px; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; transition: border-color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), transform 220ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), box-shadow 220ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-card-title { font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 600; color: #171717; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 1rem; color: #404040; line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Interactive Card
components.description: Clickable card. Hover lifts 1px, border strengthens, ambient shadow appears.
components.html:
Campaign Builder

Practice building a Sponsored Products campaign end to end.
components.css: .ds-card-interactive { cursor: pointer; } .ds-card-interactive:hover { border-color: #D4D4D4; transform: translateY(-1px); box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.04), 0 1px 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.02); } .ds-card-interactive:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #FF6B35; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Default form field. Orange border and soft ring on focus.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-label { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #404040; letter-spacing: 0.01em; } .ds-input { height: 40px; padding: 0 14px; background: #FFFFFF; border: 1.5px solid #E5E5E0; border-radius: 6px; color: #171717; outline: none; transition: border-color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), box-shadow 120ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #D4D4D4; } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: #FF6B35; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px #FFE5D9; }

components.name: Accent Badge
components.description: Status pill — soft background, saturated text, never the reverse.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; height: 22px; padding: 0 12px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; background: #FFE5D9; color: #FF6B35; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Fixed sidebar nav row. Active state: soft orange background, orange text, left-border indicator.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 12px; border-radius: 6px; color: #404040; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; text-decoration: none; border-left: 2px solid transparent; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: #F4F3EE; color: #171717; } .ds-nav-item-active { background: #FFE5D9; color: #FF6B35; border-left-color: #FF6B35; font-weight: 600; } .ds-nav-icon { width: 20px; height: 20px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 10px; }

narrative.overview:
This system reads like a 1970s technical service manual or a Federal Express waybill, not a SaaS landing page: dense, hierarchical, typographically clear, built for someone who needs to find one specific piece of information under pressure. Every screen assumes the reader is a tired VA on a phone at 2am who needs to find the quiz retry button — not a visitor who needs to be sold, delighted, or slowed down with hero copy. Density is the point, not a compromise.

The palette stays disciplined on purpose: warm paper neutrals and near-black ink carry almost every surface, and Waybill Orange is spent like a rationed resource — one accent element per viewport, two at the absolute limit. Borders do the elevation work that shadows do everywhere else; nothing floats or glows without a state (hover, focus, error) earning it.

This system explicitly rejects glassmorphism with gradient orbs, cyan-on-dark neon, oversized-hero portfolio layouts with 80% white space, and the generic "AI-built SaaS" template look. It is not decorated. It is used.
keyCharacteristics: Warm off-white paper base, never pure white as the app background
One disciplined accent (Waybill Orange), spent sparingly and deliberately
Borders as elevation; shadows only as a response to interaction, never at rest
Space Grotesk for everything read as language; JetBrains Mono for everything read as data
Motion limited to three moves: fade, slide-up, color transition — nothing else

rules.name: The Rationed Accent Rule
rules.body: Waybill Orange appears on at most two elements per viewport. Its rarity is what makes it readable as "this matters" — spread it across a whole screen and it stops meaning anything.

rules.name: The Contrast-Over-Convention Rule
rules.body: Primary-button text is dark ink on orange (#1A1A2E, 6.0:1 contrast), not the "obvious" white — white on this orange fails AA (2.83:1). Check contrast, don't assume the convention.

rules.name: The One Display Rule
rules.body: Exactly one display-sized element exists per page — the marketing hero. Everything else, no matter how important, is headline or smaller.

rules.name: The No-900-Weight Rule
rules.body: Nothing goes above weight 700. Heaviness is signaled by scale and spacing, not by maxing out the weight axis.

rules.name: The Border-Is-Elevation Rule
rules.body: A static card, input, or panel is distinguished from its background by a 1px border, never a shadow. Shadow appears only when something is actively lifted.

dos: Do keep the app background warm off-white (#FAFAF7), never pure white — pure white is reserved for cards and panels sitting on top of it.
Do spend Waybill Orange like a rationed resource: one accent element per viewport, two maximum.
Do use a 1px border as the elevation signal for any static card, input, or panel.
Do reserve shadows for elements actually being lifted by interaction — never at rest.
Do pair every error and success state with text + icon + color, never color alone.
Do keep tap targets at least 44×44px on mobile, and every focus state a visible 2px Waybill Orange ring.
Do cap line length at 72ch for lesson body text, 60ch for UI copy.
donts: Don't use glassmorphism, gradient orbs, or backdrop-blur decoration.
Don't build oversized hero sections with 80% white space in the portfolio-site style.
Don't reach for the generic "AI-built SaaS" template look: gradient-text headlines, identical icon-card grids, hero-metric stat blocks.
Don't use font weight 800 or 900 anywhere.
Don't put white text on the primary accent button — it fails AA contrast.
Don't add a shadow to a card, input, or panel that isn't actively being interacted with.
